Speaking Topics

What Ville speaks about

01

Enterprise AI Governance

Organizations adopted AI faster than they learned to manage it. The challenge is no longer adoption. It is governance. Ville breaks down how enterprises build the infrastructure, visibility, and control needed to run AI at production scale without losing sleep.

02

The Future of Work and Leadership

What if every employee thought like a CEO? Ville's TEDx talk turned conventional management thinking upside down. This keynote challenges leaders to build cultures where ownership is distributed, Mondays don't suck, and performance follows purpose.

03

Building and Scaling Tech Companies

Three companies built, three INC5000 lists, three U.S. acquisitions. Ville shares the real mechanics of scaling a software company from zero to eight figures, including what breaks, what holds, and what most founders get wrong before they figure it out.

04

Practical AI Adoption

Most AI strategies fail at the implementation layer. Ville brings the operator's perspective, moving beyond the hype to show how teams actually integrate AI into real products, real workflows, and real organizations with measurable outcomes and managed risk.

About

Finnish engineer.
American entrepreneur.

Ville Houttu is a serial founder, CEO, and keynote speaker based in Arizona. Originally from Finland, he moved to California in 2016 to build Vincit's U.S. subsidiary from scratch, growing it to an eight-figure exit and earning recognition on the INC5000 Fastest Growing Companies list three years in a row.

He has spoken on the TEDx stage, been featured on Adam Grant's WorkLife podcast, and his work has appeared in Forbes, TechCrunch, Inc. Magazine, and Harvard Business Review. He was a finalist in the Nordic Business Forum Speaker Contest and the 2023 EY Entrepreneur of the Year award.

Today he leads A-CX, a software engineering and AI company that helps enterprises design, build, and govern AI at scale through A-CX AI Nexus. He holds an MS in Engineering and coaches high school mountain biking when he's not racing single-speed in the desert.
